title: Native Editors: Obsidian + VS Code parity + interconnection (Rust)

markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection generated.

build_target.note: CANONICAL build target for ALL WP-012 MTs - do not drift. The first MT-authoring workflow fabricated crate names (handshake_frontend / handshake_native_editors / handshake_native_frontend) and a nonexistent folder tree (src/frontend/code_editor|panes|panels|shared|interop|fems, tests/native_editors), none of which exist. The audit (wf_29d00c37-f8b) caught it; all 74 MTs were re-anchored to this real crate + flat module layout (wf_775937ef-e81, 2026-06-21: 346 crate refs + 734 paths corrected) and deterministically verified clean (0 fabricated crate names, 0 fabricated paths, contract-check OK). MTs REUSE existing WP-011 modules by their real flat names (command_registry.rs, command_palette.rs, event_bus.rs, pane_registry.rs, pane_header.rs, canvas_board.rs, loom_graph.rs, settings_dialog.rs, workspace_settings.rs, context_menu.rs, context_menu_surfaces.rs, search_rail.rs, top_menu_bar.rs, quick_switcher.rs, source_control.rs, backend_client.rs, layout_persistence.rs, split_layout.rs, tab_bar.rs, accessibility/*, mcp/*, theme/*, quiet_mode/*) and CREATE new editor modules under code_root.

operator_execution_order.order: Operator 2026-06-19: rebuild the Obsidian + VS Code clones as native Rust Handshake tools with FULL feature parity to the existing React surfaces, interconnected with EACH OTHER and with CKC + Loom. Hosted in the WP-KERNEL-011 native shell. Reuse the existing Rust backend (do not rebuild it). As many MTs as needed, each executable by a small no-context local model. Minimal governance paperwork.

operator_execution_order_expansion.order: Operator 2026-06-21: Handshake hosts Obsidian+VSCode parity under the Notes pillar (not stitched-on apps). The editors MUST also work together with CKC, FEMS, Loom and other pillars - if features or pillar interactions are missing, create extra microtasks. Continue the WP-KERNEL-011 GUI so every surface has a sensible worksurface; place the correct items in the settings menu and keep it structured. Create the WP worktree + backup so implementation can start in fresh sessions. Operator rule recorded: WP worktrees base on main for the code side (gov_kernel as .GOV symlink), never on another WP's feature branch.

scope.summary:
Rebuild, as native Rust Handshake tools hosted in the WP-KERNEL-011 shell, the two editor surfaces that currently exist as React (KERNEL-009 'Notes'): (1) the VS-Code-class CODE editor and (2) the Obsidian/Notion-class RICH-TEXT + knowledge editor. Achieve FULL feature parity with what KERNEL-009 already delivered in React, then make them deeply interconnect with each other and with CKC (Pillar 18/19 + KERNEL-005) and Loom (Pillar 7). REUSE the existing Rust backend (handshake_core: knowledge documents, loom, code-nav, CRDT, search) - do NOT rebuild backend storage/APIs. The legacy React editors (app/src) stay as the parity reference until native parity is proven, then are retired. No new future surfaces (Photoshop/Affinity/spreadsheet/engines) are built here; the editors must expose clean extension seams so those plug in later. EXPANSION 2026-06-21: the editors are the Notes pillar (native, not stitched-on apps) and MUST melt together with FEMS (Pillar 12 typed memory), Stage (Pillar 17), Calendar (Pillar 2), and Locus (Pillar 6) in addition to CKC + Loom (E9/E10); continue the WP-KERNEL-011 GUI by wiring the disabled editor-orbit shell placeholders to the real editors (E11); and complete a structured Settings menu + built-in manual for the editors (E12). All pillar-interop is backend-reuse-only (typed blockers for gaps).

[SCOPE ADJUSTMENT 2026-06-27] This WP is ALSO expanded to build Handshake+'+s three-tier diagnostic substrate decoupled from the Flight Recorder (v02.196 §5.8 internal_diagnostics + §6.13 Palmistry), as MT-081..MT-096 on the same ultracode pipeline. internal_diagnostics = in-app self-diagnostics; Palmistry = external out-of-process watcher that survives freezes/crashes; FR kept as-is (no schema re-open). Records NO project/sensitive data (typed allowlist).

[SCOPE EXPANSION #2 2026-06-27] This WP is FURTHER expanded (MT-097..MT-104) to make Handshake usable as the product: a notes work-surface with a chatbox beside it (Obsidian + VS Code/Cursor/Antigravity model), with the non-notes feature windows stripped from the default layout for now (rewired per-feature later); plus terminal + local/cloud model launch inside a repo folder with the correct wrapper; plus a working visual debugger + FOREGROUND-SAFE front-end navigation (no operator mouse/keyboard steal) so models can test in-app; plus the internal manual. Built AFTER the diagnostic MTs 081-096.
forbidden_paths: app/src/** (legacy React editors - parity reference only, do not edit)
src/backend/** (reuse via existing API/IPC only; backend changes are out of scope and must be raised as a typed blocker)
spec_anchors: Product Reference Pillar 3 (Monaco/code), Pillar 4 (Word/rich-text), Pillar 7 (Loom), Pillar 18/19 (Studio/Atelier/CKC)
Product Reference Pillar 12 (FEMS memory), Pillar 17 (Stage), Pillar 2 (Calendar), Pillar 6 (Locus)
Master Spec 11.5.13 FR-EVT-MEM-001..005; Appendix 12.6 IMX force-multiplier edges
WP-KERNEL-009 delivered feature set (parity source: MT-244..264 and the Loom/Notes MT family)
acceptance_criteria: CODE editor reaches parity with the existing React Monaco surface: syntax highlighting (tree-sitter), large-file performance, multi-cursor/column-select, find/replace, code folding, minimap/outline, go-to-line, and LSP (completion/hover/go-to-def/references/diagnostics). Proven against the same scenarios the React version passed.

RICH-TEXT editor reaches parity with the existing React Tiptap surface: block document model, headings/lists/tables/quotes/code-blocks, embeds (image/slideshow/album/video), wikilinks + transclusion, slash commands, properties panel, find/replace, daily notes, and save-to-format (HTML/md/txt/JSON).

Obsidian-class knowledge surface parity: local + global graph, folder tree + color labels, tags + tag hubs, pins/favorites/backlinks/unlinked, breadcrumbs, wiki-page projection, canvas board placing real Loom blocks, and block-collection views (table/Kanban/calendar).
Search parity: Loom Search v2 surface (full-text + fuzzy + semantic, graph-blended, results-as-saved-view), find-in-files, and quick-switcher integration - all bound to the existing backend.

INTERCONNECTION (the core deliverable): the two editors and CKC and Loom melt together - shared selection/clipboard/command bus across surfaces; everything is addressable as a Loom block with live backlinks; CKC media/characters/moodboards can be embedded/dragged into notes and canvas and routed to Stage; code and notes can cross-reference; one unified undo + one event-ledger across surfaces. Proven by interconnection scenario tests.

Backend reuse proven: editors read/write through the EXISTING handshake_core APIs (knowledge documents, loom, code-nav, CRDT) with no backend rewrite; any backend gap is a typed blocker, not an in-WP backend change.
Model-vision parity: every editor + knowledge action is exposed via the WP-011 AccessKit surface so a swarm agent can author/edit code and notes and drive the graph/canvas by stable id; proven.

Full-parity proof suite: a runtime suite maps each KERNEL-009-delivered feature to a passing native proof (the parity gate); performance proven on large docs; interconnection proven; zero placeholders/mocks for spec-required runtime behavior.

EXPANSION - Code-editor VS Code parity completed and proven against the parity gate: signature help, rename symbol via WorkspaceEdit, code actions / quick-fix lightbulb, format document/selection, line-edit buffer transforms behind the keymap, go-to next/prev diagnostic + navigate back/forward, in-file symbol jump + sticky scroll, and bracket-match/colorization + indent guides + word wrap (MT-047..MT-054).

EXPANSION - Rich-text and Obsidian knowledge parity completed and proven: reading/preview mode, heading outline/TOC, wikilink alias resolution + create-from-unresolved-link, inline #tag authoring, wiki-page markdown rendering, graph filter/group/depth/color controls, outgoing-links pane, and canvas card resize + labeled sections + in-place edit (MT-055..MT-062).

EXPANSION - FEMS interop (Pillar 12) proven against real PostgreSQL/EventLedger: editors consume the MemoryPack/retrieval capsule inline with provenance and emit review-gated memory-write proposals (FR-EVT-MEM-001) - NEVER editor-direct commits; backend gaps surface as typed blockers (MT-063..MT-065).

EXPANSION - Other-named-pillar interop proven end-to-end: Stage (Pillar 17) route + embed-back with manifest provenance, Calendar (Pillar 2) daily-note<->CalendarEvent binding + read-only ActivitySpan correlation, and Locus (Pillar 6) locus:// WP/MT cross-references, each AccessKit-steerable and emitting expected FR events (MT-066..MT-068, MT-074).

EXPANSION - WP-011 GUI worksurface continuation proven: the menu items, command-palette entries, and context menus WP-011 delivered as honestly-disabled editor placeholders now fire real WP-012 editor actions through the existing shell state-mutation paths, plus VS Code-class status-bar editor segments (language mode, EOL, indent, encoding, whitespace) - no new shell infrastructure forked (MT-069..MT-071).

EXPANSION - Structured settings + menu completion + manual delivered: editor-specific Settings sections persist via the existing PostgreSQL-backed /workspaces/:id/settings surface, and the built-in User Manual documents the editor workflows and every agent AccessKit author_id + MCP swarm tool (HBR-MAN) (MT-072..MT-073).

EXPANSION - All interop MTs treat any missing handshake_core endpoint (FEMS pack/proposals, Stage embed-back, Calendar activity-spans, Locus read) as a TYPED BLOCKER with a visible empty-state and a no-silent-no-op test; zero backend edits and zero SQLite anywhere.

interconnection_contract.principle: CX-003-VIS / CX-004-VIS: everything melts together, everything accesses everything; handmade primitives share one selection, undo, event ledger, and model-accessibility model.
edges: RichText <-> Code: open a code block/file from a note in the code editor; reference code symbols from notes; shared find/replace and command bus.
Editors <-> Loom: every document/block/canvas node is a Loom block with content hash + backlinks; Loom Search v2 indexes all of it; AI Loom jobs propose tags/links across surfaces.

Editors <-> CKC (Studio/Atelier/KERNEL-005): drag CKC media/characters/worldbuilding/moodboards into a note or canvas as embeds; route a note/selection to a Stage pane; CKC artifacts appear in the same library and search.

Shared substrate: one selection model, one clipboard, one command palette/registry, one undo stack scope policy, one event ledger (Flight Recorder), one AccessKit model-surface across all editor panes.

Editors <-> FEMS (Pillar 12): consume the retrieval capsule / MemoryPack inline (provenance-first, <=500 tokens, <=24 items); emit review-gated memory-write proposals with document_id + selection-range + content-hash provenance (FR-EVT-MEM-001). Commit stays review-gated, never editor-direct.
Editors <-> Stage (Pillar 17): route a note/selection/canvas-node to a Stage pane; embed Stage capture artifacts back into notes/canvas as MT-014 embeds with SHA-256 manifest provenance.
Editors <-> Calendar (Pillar 2): daily notes bind to CalendarEvents by date; read-only ActivitySpan correlation surfaces which documents were edited during a calendar block.
Editors <-> Locus (Pillar 6): notes and code cross-reference WP/MT records via locus:// addressing reusing the everything-is-a-block scheme, with reverse lookup of referencing documents.
Editors <-> WP-011 shell: all editor actions route through the existing menu / command bus / unified undo / context-menu / status-bar / settings / manual substrate (extension, not fork).
Future extension seams (designed, NOT built): image/photo editor (Photoshop/Affinity), spreadsheet, mechanical engines - all attach to the same block/selection/event/accessibility substrate.

mt_plan.note: Cluster plan. Each MT-*.json is authored per template and must be executable by a small no-context local model. Toolkit primitives reference the WP-KERNEL-011 MT-001 decision (egui+egui_tiles recommended). Parity is measured against the KERNEL-009 delivered feature set. 46 base MTs (E1-E8) authored 2026-06-19; 28 expansion MTs (MT-047..MT-074: E1/E2/E3 parity fill-ins + E9 FEMS + E10 Stage/Calendar/Locus + E11 GUI continuation + E12 settings/manual) authored 2026-06-21. +4 i18n MTs (MT-075..MT-078: E13 Internationalization/Unicode/input - CJK font coverage, IME inline preedit, UAX#14/#29 segmentation, RTL/bidi) authored by KERNEL_BUILDER 2026-06-22 as an operator-ordered scope expansion after a code inspection found Latin-only font registration (no CJK). All 78 preserved (CX-653A).

clusters.name: Code editor (VS Code parity)
clusters.summary: Text buffer (ropey) + tree-sitter highlighting; large-file virtualization; multi-cursor/column-select; find/replace + replace-in-file; code folding; minimap/outline + go-to-line; gutter (diagnostics/breakpoints); LSP client (completion/hover/def/refs/diagnostics); diff/merge editor; Monaco-parity keymap. EXPANSION: signature help, rename symbol (WorkspaceEdit), code actions/quick-fix lightbulb, format document/selection, line-edit transforms, diagnostic nav + jump history, in-file symbol jump + sticky scroll, bracket/indent/word-wrap chrome.

clusters.name: Rich-text editor (Obsidian/Notion parity)
clusters.summary: Block document model (ProseMirror-style on ropey) + cosmic-text WYSIWYG; headings/lists/tables/quotes/code-blocks; embeds (image/slideshow/album/video NodeViews); wikilinks + transclusion; slash commands; properties panel; find/replace; daily-notes/journal; save-to-format (HTML/md/txt/JSON); draft/crash recovery. EXPANSION: reading/preview mode, heading outline/TOC, wikilink alias resolve + create-from-unresolved-link, inline #tags, wiki-page markdown rendering.

clusters.name: Knowledge surface / Obsidian graph (Loom)
clusters.summary: Local + global graph view; folder tree + color labels; tags + tag hubs; pins/favorites/backlinks/unlinked; breadcrumbs; wiki-page projection + editable overlay; canvas board placing real Loom blocks; block-collection views (table/Kanban/calendar). EXPANSION: graph filter/group/depth/color controls, canvas card resize/sections/inline-edit, outgoing-links pane.

clusters.name: Search surfaces
clusters.summary: Loom Search v2 native surface (full-text+fuzzy+semantic, graph-blended, faceted, results-as-saved-view); find-in-files (+replace-in-files preview); quick-switcher integration (open docs/blocks/symbols/MTs/WPs/manual/wiki).

clusters.name: Interconnection / melt-together
clusters.summary: Shared selection + clipboard + command bus across editors; everything-is-a-Loom-block addressing + live backlinks; CKC embeds/drag-in (media/characters/moodboards) + route-to-Stage; code<->note cross-references; one unified undo scope policy; one event-ledger (Flight Recorder) across surfaces; designed extension seams for image/spreadsheet/engines.

clusters.name: Backend reuse wiring
clusters.summary: Bind native editors to EXISTING handshake_core APIs: knowledge documents CRUD/history/projection; loom blocks/graph/tags/views; code-nav (symbols/refs/file-lens); CRDT push/pull/conflict. No backend rewrite; gaps raised as typed blockers.

clusters.name: Model-vision/steering parity
clusters.summary: Expose every editor + knowledge + search action through the WP-011 AccessKit surface with stable author_ids; a swarm agent can create/edit a note and code and drive graph/canvas by id; in-process snapshot + action proof.

clusters.name: Parity + interconnection proof
clusters.summary: Parity proof suite mapping each KERNEL-009 delivered feature to a passing native runtime proof (the parity gate); large-document performance proof; interconnection scenario proofs (CKC->note embed, note<->code, Loom backlink/search across surfaces).

clusters.name: FEMS interop (typed memory pillar)
clusters.summary: Editors interoperate with FEMS (Pillar 12 typed memory: episodic/semantic/procedural + retrieval capsule). Editors CONSUME the MemoryPack/retrieval capsule inline (provenance-first, <=500 tokens/<=24 items) and GENERATE review-gated memory-write proposals from editing context (FR-EVT-MEM-001), NEVER direct commits. Reuses existing handshake_core FEMS APIs (typed blocker if absent); proven end-to-end against real PG (MT-065).

clusters.name: Other-pillar interop (Stage / Calendar / Locus)
clusters.summary: Editors melt together with named product pillars beyond CKC/Loom: Stage (Pillar 17, route-to-stage + embed-back capture artifacts with manifest provenance), Calendar (Pillar 2, daily-note<->CalendarEvent binding + ActivitySpan correlation), and Locus (Pillar 6, locus:// WP/MT cross-references). Reuses existing backend read/route APIs (typed blockers for gaps) and the E5 addressing/navigation substrate; proven end-to-end (MT-074).

clusters.name: WP-011 GUI worksurface continuation
clusters.summary: Connect the WP-011 shell surfaces delivered as honestly-disabled editor placeholders to the now-real WP-012 editors, reusing the established shell state-mutation paths: wire menu items + enable disabled command-palette editor entries; wire editor/canvas context menus + complete the NavigationTarget bus; add VS Code-class status-bar editor segments (language mode, EOL, indent, encoding, whitespace). No new shell infrastructure is forked.

clusters.name: Structured settings + menu completion + manual
clusters.summary: Complete the operator/agent-facing chrome the GUI doc defines for the editors on top of the WP-011 settings dialog + manual pane: editor-specific Settings sections (editor font/tab/wrap/whitespace, syntax palette editor, editor keybindings) persisted through the existing PostgreSQL-backed settings surface; and the built-in User Manual editor content plus the agent-vision/steering AccessKit+MCP tool reference (HBR-MAN). Reuses WP-011 persistence and manual infrastructure.

clusters.name: Internationalization / Unicode / input (CJK, Cyrillic, symbols, RTL)
clusters.summary: Operator-ordered scope expansion 2026-06-22 (full language/symbol/character coverage). Code inspection found install_fonts registers ONLY Inter (Latin+Cyrillic+Greek) + no CJK, so Chinese/Japanese/Korean render as tofu and there is no CJK input/segmentation/RTL. MT-075 builds an ordered egui font fallback chain (CJK + symbols + emoji glyph coverage); MT-076 makes IME composition first-class with inline underlined preedit in both editors (closes the MT-012 AC-6 partial) + set_ime_allowed; MT-077 adds Unicode-correct text segmentation (UAX#14 CJK line-break, UAX#29 grapheme caret + word/char counts); MT-078 adds RTL (Hebrew/Arabic) bidi reordering (unicode-bidi) + a HONEST tiered scope for complex-script shaping (typed limitation, never silently-broken Arabic). Pure frontend (no backend); rope stays logical-order so backend round-trip is unaffected.

clusters.name: Drift-audit re-scope + residual hardening (2026-07-02)
clusters.summary: MT-107 re-scopes the backend launch-contract validator + managed-PG/artifact-root remediation that rode into recovery commit 3bf307a8 under MT-101 (forbidden-path breach disclosure per GLOBAL-SCOPE-005). MT-108 collects wave-A audit minors as a tracked residual-hardening backlog so approved-minor gaps are not lost.

mt_plan.kernel_builder_sequence_note: KERNEL_BUILDER re-sequence 2026-06-24: the E8 proof capstones MT-044 (parity suite, depends_on 001-043+047-062), MT-045 (perf proof), MT-046 (interconnection proof) are DEFERRED to the END of the WP - they prove the whole editor set + most of their proofs need a live managed PostgreSQL (NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF). Build the remaining FEATURE MTs first in numeric order: E1-deepening 047-054, E2-deepening 055-059, E3-deepening 060-062, E9 063-065, E10 066-068+074, E11 host-mount 069-071 (the convergence point that mounts the editors live + clears the carry-forwards), E12 072-073, E13 i18n 075-078. THEN run the 044-046 capstones last for a truthful comprehensive parity manifest.
mt_plan.diagnostic_expansion_note: WP SCOPE ADJUSTMENT (operator 2026-06-27): MT-081..MT-096 added to THIS WP build the three-tier diagnostic tools decoupled from the Flight Recorder (Master Spec v02.196 §5.8 internal_diagnostics + §6.13 Palmistry): internal_diagnostics (in-app native-egui self-diagnostics: shm ring, panic hook, heartbeat, frame-time, CPU/RSS/GPU, open diagnostic-event API, in-app panel, backend-down freeze fix) + Palmistry (external out-of-process watcher: ring reader, freeze/crash detection, minidump, survivor store, FR-forward). FR kept as-is. Same ultracode per-MT pipeline as the editor MTs. MT-081=ring substrate; 082-088=internal_diagnostics; 089-094=Palmistry; 095=HBR-INT-009 gate; 096=end-to-end freeze+crash proof. The retrofit of already-built features = WP-KERNEL-016 stub (future).
mt_plan.usability_expansion_note: WP SCOPE EXPANSION #2 (operator 2026-06-27): MT-097..MT-104 = make Handshake actually USABLE as the product - a work surface with a chatbox beside it (like Obsidian + VS Code/Cursor/Antigravity; Handshake goes deeper later with more tools/workflows). Operator currently CANNOT use notes in the running GUI. MT-097 notes-ONLY default worksurface (strip/hide all non-notes feature panes for now - add+rewire per-feature later); MT-098 chatbox panel beside the worksurface (the editor+chat core layout); MT-099 end-to-end notes usability (open->edit->save a document in the running app, proven live); MT-100 terminal-session launch inside a repo folder with the correct wrapper; MT-101 local/cloud MODEL launch inside a repo folder with the correct wrapper; MT-102 visual debugger inspects operator worksurfaces/window-structure/tooling; MT-103 foreground-SAFE front-end navigation (drive+test the GUI WITHOUT stealing operator mouse/keyboard) - patch+expand so models can test inside Handshake; MT-104 internal manual updated for notes/editors + terminal/model launch + visual debugger. ORDER: built AFTER the diagnostic MTs 081-096. The VS Code/Obsidian clone is a main selling ticket - inaccessible = broken. DIAGNOSTICS PLACEMENT (operator 2026-06-27): the in-app diagnostics panel (MT-087) lives in the OPTIONS/SETTINGS window (Settings->Diagnostics), NOT as a worksurface feature pane - so MT-097 notes-only worksurface excludes it and MT-104 manual documents it under Settings.
mt_plan.preexisting_suite_failures: As of MT-085 full-suite: (1) test_menu_wireup::file_save (MT-069/020 FILE>Save dispatch) -> notes-save broken, pinned MT-099; (2) test_quick_switcher nav/focus kittest flakiness (MT-017/030) -> open-file nav, check in MT-097/099; (3) test_perf_large_code::perf_lc07_minimap (MT-045) -> -j2 concurrency flake, passes isolated. All baseline-proven pre-existing (NOT diagnostic-build regressions).
mt_plan.diagnostic_gate_policy: DIAGNOSTIC MT GATE (from MT-086 on): a diagnostic MT (081-096) is gated on (a) its OWN feature tests GREEN + (b) NO NEW full-suite regression vs the feat/WP-KERNEL-012 baseline (proven by baseline-stash isolation), NOT on a fully-green -j2 suite. The pre-existing editor-MT flakes are a documented carve-out (WP-level test-stabilization debt): test_menu_wireup::file_save (MT-069/020, FILE>Save - real bug, pinned MT-099), test_quick_switcher (MT-017/030, non-deterministic kittest timing), test_settings_dialog::reset_panes (kittest timing), test_perf_large_code::perf_lc07 (MT-045, -j2 concurrency flake, passes isolated). These are owned by their editor MTs + the usability cluster (097-099) / WP validation to stabilize.
mt_plan.palmistry_test_bound_policy: MT-092 PRECEDENT (hard rule for MT-093/094/096): ANY test that spawns the palmistry binary, does minidumper-IPC, or waits on a child MUST be hard-bounded (wait_for_exit/connect/read with an explicit timeout - NEVER an unbounded Command::output() or socket read_line). #[ignore] with a typed reason any test that deadlocks under this headless/sandboxed harness (minidumper out-of-process IPC + simulate_exception are the known hangers; run them with --ignored on a real host). The coder must run palmistry tests under a hard external bound. This prevents the multi-hour hang MT-092 hit.
mt_plan.stall_detection_expansion_note: WP SCOPE EXPANSION #3 (operator 2026-06-28): MT-105/106 = make the hours-long SILENT HANG that just wasted operator time IMPOSSIBLE inside Handshake. Generalize freeze detection from Handshake+'+s own UI thread to ANY operation + spawned subprocess, caught by BOTH internal_diagnostics and Palmistry. MT-105 internal_diagnostics OPERATION/SUBPROCESS STALL WATCHDOG: any in-app operation (backend call, spawned terminal/model session MT-100/101, tool run, workflow step) registers a deadline + periodic progress heartbeat; a stall past the deadline WITHOUT progress emits a typed StalledOperation diagnostic + surfaces it (Settings->Diagnostics + status) within a BOUNDED time - never silent. MT-106 Palmistry CHILD-PROCESS STALL WATCH + end-to-end NO-SILENT-HANG proof: Palmistry watches the child processes Handshake SPAWNS (sessions/tools/terminals/models), detecting a hung child (alive but no progress) the same way it detects Handshake+'+s freeze + surfacing it; the capstone proof spawns a DELIBERATELY-hung operation AND a deliberately-hung child and asserts internal_diagnostics emits StalledOperation AND Palmistry surfaces the hung child, BOTH within a bounded time (seconds, not hours). ORDER: implemented AFTER the current diagnostic (081-096) + usability (097-104) MTs; created now so state is not lost. depends_on: 105 -> internal_diagnostics tier (082-088); 106 -> Palmistry (089-094) + 105.
mt_plan.pipeline_speed_note: Lightened pipeline 2026-06-28 (operator: too much time): per-MT tests scoped to the TOUCHED crate + clippy + build; the full ~90-bin no-regression suite runs ONCE at WP validation, not per-MT. Anti-hang rules baked in (no windowed-app launch in tests, all child/IPC tests bounded). Watchdog bgsw0lgfb auto-kills runaway apps.
mt_plan.status_sync_note_2026_07_01: Tracker refreshed after operator correction. WP is active on branch feat/WP-KERNEL-012, worktree ../wtc-native-editors-v1. MT-101 is reopened for active rework. The reviewer gaps around workflow_run_id UI/status binding and duplicate-submit request proof were patched and proven by cargo test --manifest-path src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-native-target --test test_model_session_launch: 11 passed, 0 failed. reconciliation_debt.note: Master Spec product-surface text lags the native rebuild in three places to reconcile in a LATER spec pass (NOT this WP): (a) Product Reference Tech Stack + Pillars 3/4 still name React/Tiptap/Monaco/Tauri vs the native Rust egui+egui_tiles+AccessKit+wgpu (no-webview) frontend; (b) Master Spec 10-product-surfaces (~412/441/444) mandates DOCX import/export via AST + dual RichView+Monaco StructuredTextView that WP-012 scopes OUT (parked to WP-KERNEL-014; operator decision needed - do not silently drop); (c) FR-EVT-002 (editor) producer mapping vs MT-036 'system' event_type + nested native_editor payload - confirm/reconcile the native-editor FR producer mapping.

future_wp_stubs.reason: Deferred Notion-class scope beyond React parity: user-editable template engine, typed page properties (select/multi-select/date/number/checkbox/status), richer database views (relations/rollups).

Spec-mandated DOCX import/export via AST + dual RichView+Monaco StructuredTextView, scoped OUT of WP-012 React-parity export (HTML/MD/TXT/JSON); requires operator decision per reconciliation debt (b).

assumptions_to_attack: A native Rust rich-text WYSIWYG can reach Tiptap parity (hardest gap; ProseMirror-style model is greenfield - validate early with a vertical slice).
A native code editor can reach Monaco parity incl. LSP + multi-cursor + large files (validate with the heaviest real files).
Interconnection is assumed; prove cross-surface selection/undo/event-ledger and CKC/Loom edges with real scenarios.
Backend reuse is assumed sufficient; any gap must surface as a typed blocker, not a silent backend edit.
Parity is asserted vs proven; every KERNEL-009 feature must map to a passing native proof.
EXPANSION: FEMS memory writes must NEVER be editor-direct commits - MT-064 enforces review-gated proposals only; a direct-commit path is a red-team failure.
EXPANSION: FEMS/Stage/Calendar/Locus backend endpoints are assumed present; each interop MT must carry a typed-blocker + visible empty-state + no-silent-no-op test.
EXPANSION: WP-011 substrate must be reused not forked; E11/E12 tests assert routing through the existing shared state-mutation paths.
minimum_controls: Parity gate: a runtime proof per KERNEL-009-delivered feature AND per new VS Code/Obsidian parity MT; no placeholders/mocks for spec-required runtime behavior.
Rich-text and code editors each ship an early vertical-slice proof before full cluster build.
Interconnection edges proven by scenario tests, not asserted; MT-065 (FEMS) + MT-074 (Stage/Calendar/Locus) interop proof suites required.
Backend untouched; gaps are typed blockers; zero SQLite anywhere.
MT-064 grep gate: no direct memory-commit call site; review_gated hard-true for Procedural class.
